concerns.text: FR-003 mandates verifying differentiability by ensuring gradients flow 'through the decoder' to the solver parameters. Task T019b only mentions ensuring gradients flow 'to the solver parameters', omitting the critical 'through the decoder' verification step required by the spec and Constitution Principle VI.

SC-001 defines success as reaching the target zone (center within 5cm) without collision for one second. Task T021b only implements logic to measure 'one-second collision-free duration', missing the explicit verification of the 'target zone' (manipulation completed) component of the success criteria.

SC-002 requires comparing latency on the 'same hardware configuration'. Task T022a (baseline GAM script) does not explicitly mandate running on CPU-only hardware or freezing weights, risking a comparison against a GPU-accelerated or fine-tuned baseline which would invalidate the latency metric.

The spec's Assumption about computational limits and the plan's performance goal state the solver must solve within '<300 seconds per step'. Task T031 sets a target of '<300ms/step', a 1000x stricter constraint not found in the spec, which may make the 6-hour CI window impossible to meet and contradicts the experimental design.

Task T019b description contains a stale or incorrect reference to 'solver parameters' as the sole gradient target, whereas the spec (FR-003) and Constitution (Principle VI) require gradients to flow through the decoder. This is a writing error in the task description that misrepresents the spec's requirement.
